content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Troubleshooting Your Offline Equipment

  - url: https://www.spectrum.net/support/internet/troubleshooting-your-offline-equipment

  - source: Spectrum

  - domain: spectrum.net


  ## Content


  # Troubleshooting Your Offline Equipment


  Use this support process when Spectrum equipment is showing as offline or is not
  maintaining a working connection.


  Start with the physical connections. Check that the equipment has power and that
  its cables are firmly connected. Make sure the coaxial cable is attached securely
  and that other connection points have not come loose.


  If the equipment remains offline, restart it. Disconnect the equipment from power,
  leave it unplugged for about 60 seconds, and then reconnect it. Allow time for the
  equipment to start again and re-establish its connection before deciding whether
  the problem remains.


  Repeat the connection checks after the restart if the equipment still does not return
  online. A loose power, coaxial, or other service connection can prevent the equipment
  from reconnecting normally.


  This page is an equipment-troubleshooting procedure. Its steps focus on the status
  and connections of Spectrum equipment and on restarting offline equipment. Follow
  the equipment checks before moving to unrelated account, billing, or general-contact
  topics.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Troubleshooting Your Offline Equipment
url: https://www.spectrum.net/support/internet/troubleshooting-your-offline-equipment

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Contact Spectrum Customer Service by Phone or Chat

  - url: https://www.spectrum.com/contact-spectrum

  - source: Spectrum

  - domain: spectrum.com


  ## Content


  # Contact Spectrum Customer Service by Phone or Chat


  Spectrum provides broad customer-service contact options for Internet, TV, Mobile,
  and other service questions.


  Customers can use Spectrum''s published support phone and chat channels when they
  need direct assistance. The contact page serves as a general entry point and includes
  access to different customer-service destinations.


  Because the page covers many kinds of requests, customers may use it for questions
  that are not limited to a single technical procedure. The available contact choices
  can include general customer support and service-specific help.


  This is a broad contact page rather than a page devoted to one narrow internet fault.
  For a problem that already matches a specific Spectrum support procedure, the dedicated
  procedure can provide more directly targeted steps than the general contact entry
  point.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Contact Spectrum Customer Service by Phone or Chat
url: https://www.spectrum.com/contact-spectrum

Spectrum is not currently showing an active provider-wide outage, so this is likely an equipment or connection issue.

Try these steps:

1. **Check power and cables**
   - Confirm the modem and router are plugged in and powered on.
   - Firmly reconnect the coaxial cable and Ethernet cables.
   - Check that cables aren’t damaged or loose.

2. **Restart the equipment**
   - Unplug the modem and router from power.
   - Leave them unplugged for about **60 seconds**.
   - Plug the modem back in first and wait for it to reconnect.
   - Then plug in the router and allow several minutes for Wi‑Fi to return.

3. **Test your connection**
   - Try another device.
   - If possible, connect a computer directly to the router with an Ethernet cable.
   - Check whether the issue affects Wi‑Fi only or all internet access.

If it still doesn’t work, use Spectrum’s [offline-equipment troubleshooting guide](https://www.spectrum.net/support/internet/troubleshooting-your-offline-equipment). You can also contact Spectrum through its [official phone or chat support page](https://www.spectrum.com/contact-spectrum).

args.reason: Determine whether the reported Spectrum home internet problem is an outage or an individual connection issue so I can give the appropriate next steps.

final_answer:
Spectrum is not currently showing an active provider-wide outage, so this is likely an equipment or connection issue.

Try these steps:

1. **Check power and cables**
   - Confirm the modem and router are plugged in and powered on.
   - Firmly reconnect the coaxial cable and Ethernet cables.
   - Check that cables aren’t damaged or loose.

2. **Restart the equipment**
   - Unplug the modem and router from power.
   - Leave them unplugged for about **60 seconds**.
   - Plug the modem back in first and wait for it to reconnect.
   - Then plug in the router and allow several minutes for Wi‑Fi to return.

3. **Test your connection**
   - Try another device.
   - If possible, connect a computer directly to the router with an Ethernet cable.
   - Check whether the issue affects Wi‑Fi only or all internet access.

If it still doesn’t work, use Spectrum’s [offline-equipment troubleshooting guide](https://www.spectrum.net/support/internet/troubleshooting-your-offline-equipment). You can also contact Spectrum through its [official phone or chat support page](https://www.spectrum.com/contact-spectrum).

text.regex:
(?isx)
^
(?=
    .*?
    (?:no|not|without)
    [^.!?\n]{0,100}
    (?:outage|service[\s_-]*outage)
)
(?=
    .*?
    (?:
        (?:equipment|modem|router)
        [^.!?\n]{0,120}
        (?:connect(?:ion|ivity)?|offline|issue|problem)
      |
        (?:equipment|connection)
        [\s_-]*(?:issue|problem)
    )
)
(?=
    .*?
    (?:
        (?:use|follow|start[\s_-]*with|try|go[\s_-]*to|recommended)
        [^.!?\n]{0,180}
    )?
    (?:official[^.!?\n]{0,100})?
    (?:spectrum[^.!?\n]{0,100})?
    (?:
        offline[\s_-]*equipment
      |
        equipment
      |
        internet[\s_-]*equipment
    )
    [\s_-]*
    (?:
        troubleshoot(?:ing)?
      |
        support[\s_-]*process
      |
        guide
      |
        page
      |
        path
    )
)
(?=
    .*?
    (?:check|inspect|secure|reconnect|tighten|verify)
    [^.!?\n]{0,120}
    (?:power|cable|coax|connection)
)
(?=
    .*?
    (?:restart|reboot|power[\s_-]*cycle|unplug)
    [^.!?\n]{0,120}
    (?:modem|router|equipment|device|them)
)
.*
$
